gate_name: BIOS/MBR boot repair with intentionally broken BCD

evidence.vm_id: SAK-PM-BIOS-MBR-Fixture-20260603-091138; pre-repair snapshot pre-bios-bcd-break-20260603-1036 UUID=bb3b4b6c-946a-422f-9517-c1d0c4e05319; offline repair runner SAK-PM-LAB
evidence.broken_boot_symptom: BCD removed from disposable MBR target W:\Boot\BCD; target boot reached Windows Recovery screen: File: \\Boot\\BCD, Error code: 0xc000000f; GuestAdditionsRunLevel stayed 0 for eight polls; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-bios/broken-20260603-104917/broken-boot-poll.json
evidence.repair_commands: bcdboot W:\Windows /s W: /f BIOS exited 0; bootsect /nt60 W: /mbr /force exited 0; bcdedit /store W:\Boot\BCD /enum all exited 0; commands_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/offline-bios-repair/20260603-110420/offline-bios-repair-commands.json
evidence.post_repair_boot_result: Passed: repaired target VM reached VMState=running, GuestAdditionsRunLevel=3, VideoMode=1024,768,32 after repair; poll_artifact=artifacts/partition-manager-certification/vm-lab/boot-repair-bios/post-repair-20260603-110420/post-repair-boot-poll.json

verification_summary: Disposable BIOS/MBR target VM was snapshotted, intentionally broken by removing W:\Boot\BCD, failed boot with Windows Recovery 0xc000000f, repaired offline with bcdboot and bootsect, and booted back to Guest Additions run level 3.
operator_notes: Only the disposable BIOS fixture disk was attached to the lab VM. Lab system disk was not selected as repair target.
